108.status: Made four rehab appearances for Single-A Rancho Cucamonga. Scratched May 10 after he didn’t bounce back as anticipated. MRI came back clean May 15, and he's eligible to return from the 60-day IL May 26.
Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Said he felt a pop and won't be able to put pressure on his foot until early June.
Manager Kurt Suzuki said Pomeranz’s placement on the IL was the Angels being “cautious.” Pomeranz said on May 15 that it's a minor injury and he expects to be back once he's eligible.
Left his outing before the start of the third inning on April 29. Underwent an MRI, will be shut down for 3-4 weeks before starting a throwing program.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Joined team in St. Petersburg, Fla., prior to series vs. Rays on May 18.
Began rehab assignment with Double-A Chesapeake on May 9 and went 1-for-6 in two games. Moved to Triple-A Norfolk on May 12 and went 4-for-18 with an RBI double in first four games.
Imaging came back clean. Took anti-inflammatory medication. Began treatment May 4. Threw for first time on May 15 at Nationals Park in Washington, then again May 16.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Expected to recover in time to return during the early part of the 2027 season.
Scratched from lineup ahead of May 12 game vs. the Yankees after feeling discomfort during pregame work. Underwent an MRI and was placed on IL the next day.
Had not pitched since May 6 in Miami. Went on injured list after not appearing in five consecutive games.
Exited May 7 start in Miami due to discomfort in left forearm. Could receive cortisone shot, though other treatment options remain on table.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return.

111.status: Exited game on May 17 because he was having trouble gripping the ball, but X-rays were negative. Narváez was feeling much better a day later and is aiming for a return on May 19.

111.expectedReturn: Possibly start of homestand on May 22
111.status: Will continue his Minor League rehab assignment with an outing at Triple-A Worcester on May 19. Could be ready to return a couple of days after that.
Placed on IL with injury that has nagged him in recent weeks. If Story has surgery, he could be out six to 10 weeks.
Threw his second side session in four days on May 16, bumping up to 35 pitches. Likely to have an up-and-down side session on May 20.

111.expectedReturn: Mid to late June at earliest
111.status: Threw a side session for first time in several weeks on May 15.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.
Has been playing catch out to 90 feet and will continue to increase volume of throws and distance as he goes.
Expected to visit a doctor soon to gauge if any progress is being made.

111.expectedReturn: No earlier than mid to late June
111.status: Will soon have consultation with Dr. Keith Meister to get better idea of where he's at.

112.status: Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 17.
Underwent left knee meniscectomy on May 7. Threw lightly off mound on May 16. Bullpen session could come during week of May 18, per manager Craig Counsell. Timeline (six weeks) unchanged.
Followed live BP on May 13 with first Minor League rehab outing on May 16. Logged 23 pitches for High-A South Bend in one scoreless inning (one hit, one walk, two strikeouts).
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per manager Craig Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 17.

Felt discomfort in recent bullpen session. Checkup with Dr. Keith Meister (who did Steele's UCL revision repair on April 18, 2025) showed a flexor strain. Shut down from throwing until asymptomatic, and will be rechecked in a month.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 1 prospect, No. 50 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain of Trevino’s left hamstring behind his knee. Terry Francona said Trevino could be back with the team in 3 to 4 weeks, though there is no exact timetable.

Made his latest appearance with Triple-A Louisville May 17, reporting that he “feels good,” according to Terry Francona. The Reds “will work though what is next” for the pitcher, whether that’s in the Majors or Minors.
Throwing out to 150 feet in Arizona. First bullpen session scheduled for May 26. If all goes well, will rejoin the team on May 29 to continue his rehab.
Met with team officials on May 13 and manager Terry Francona indicated he is making good progress to the point where he resumed throwing on May 14.

113.expectedReturn: May 17 for his next start vs. Cleveland
113.status: X-rays came back negative after taking a Daylen Lile 106.7 mph liner off the outside of his right foot. Manager Terry Francona said the team is "hopeful" Singer will make his next start.
MRI exam showed a Grade 2 strain, with estimated time away to be four to eight weeks. Starting throwing from his knees on May 8.
Went to Goodyear, Ariz., to begin rehab process on May 5. Moved to the 60-day IL on May 6, but there was no setback.

114.status: Manager Stephen Vogt noted on May 8 that Arias' rehab is "going to be a little bit longer. He hit a little snag." Was initially expected to miss four to eight weeks.

115.status: Sustained the injury during practice before May 16 game vs. Diamondbacks while practicing catching fly balls in the outfield.
Dollander left his May 14 start in the second inning with elbow tightness as he extended his arm on his fastball and changeup. An MRI showed inflammation in his elbow.
Herget pitched once after returning from the bereavement/family emergency list, but struggled and felt discomfort in the shoulder. He will be given time to calm the issue and regain his form.
Struck out three while allowing one hit in one inning on May 13 in his first injury rehab game at Triple-A Albuquerque, after two appearances each with High-A Spokane and Double-A Hartford.
Threw 35-40 pitches while facing hitters in Pittsburgh on May 13 and is scheduled to start a rehab assignment with Double-A Hartford on May 19.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Scheduled to pitch Wednesday and Thursday on rehab assignment for Triple-A Toledo. Tossed two innings and 25 pitches in second appearance for Toledo on May 15. Fastball averaged 95.9 mph, topping out at 97.2.
Cleared for more baseball activity this week, including defensive work. Left game May 9 after crashing into the wall. Diagnosed with AC joint sprain but no structural damage.
Could begin rehab assignment with Triple-A Toledo this coming week after going through a full routine of pregame work at Comerica Park.

Will make next rehab start May 19 for Single-A Lakeland, moved from Triple-A Toledo due to weather concerns. Will stretch out to four innings and 65-75 pitches. Tossed two-plus scoreless innings May 15 for Toledo.
Rejoined team in Detroit for second mound session May 18 at Comerica Park. Will remain in Detroit and settle into five-day bullpen routine.
Left game on May 15 after taking a line drive off right forearm. Initial tests indicated no fracture.
Threw 50-pitch, three-inning simulated game on May 15 at Comerica Park. Showed improvement in pitch quality and fastball velocity. Will throw a longer simulated game around middle of next week.
Threw latest bullpen session on May 15.
Began a throwing progression on May 14 after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9.

117.status: Exited May 16 game with pain in left side after a swing. An MRI on May 17 revealed the severity of the injury.
Pitched final rehab outing May 17 for Triple-A Sugar Land before joining the team in Minneapolis. Is expected to be activated sometime during the series against the Twins.

Went 0-for-4 with a run in his third Minor League rehab assignment game May 16 with Double-A Corpus Christi and is scheduled to play again May 17. There's a "strong possbility" he will join team in Minneapolis this week.
Scheduled to throw final live BP on May 19 before heading out on a Minor League rehab assignment.
Scheduled to throw an inning May 15 in a Minor League rehab outing with Double-A Corpus Christi and will need five more outings before returning.
Continuing to progress in his throwing routine and baseball activity. Could be nearing a Minor League rehab assignment the week of May 18.
Is running, hitting and throwing and could be nearing a Minor League rehab assignment during the week of May 18.
Was still experiencing soreness and was not throwing, manager Joe Espada said May 14. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. He will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.
Was scratched prior to the May 4 game against the Dodgers after suffering an injury swinging the bat in BP and placed on IL.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Felt more soreness than typical after May 14 start against White Sox. Will rest for now with expectation that it will be a short stint.
Patellar tendinitis flared up during May 15 outing in St. Louis. Will receive injection week of May 18 to help symptoms. Will keep arm in shape as much as possible during recovery.
Threw a 30-pitch bullpen in Kansas City on May 17 that went as expected. If all goes well playing catch May 18 and 19, will throw another bullpen May 19.
Suffered injury during first rehab outing on May 6 and will be shut down for three weeks. Returned from rehab assignment May 8.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 season with an elbow injury, has continued to have problems with soreness. Scheduled to see a specialist to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Expected to undergo the NanoNeedle Scope procedure on May 18, which should allow for a faster return.
Shut down from throwing after his back flared up on him again.

119.injury: Recovery from right shoulder surgery, lower back injury
119.status: Injured his lower back while on a rehab assignment and surgery could be on the table, per general manager Brandon Gomes.
Has resumed throwing and could return around three weeks after landing on the IL if all goes as planned.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Working through the early stages of a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
An MRI revealed inflammation, but no structural damage. The hope is that he won't miss much longer than the minimum 15 days.
Thinks he will be able to start playing catch a couple weeks after the stitches are removed from his elbow.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

120.status: X-rays were negative after Young was hit by a pitch in the second inning of the Nats' loss to the Mets on May 18.
Wiemer was hit on the left hand in the bottom of the ninth inning on May 18 vs. the Mets. X-rays were negative.
Rehab assignment moved to Triple-A Rochester. Allowed one hit with one strikeout over one inning on May 16. Will throw one inning on May 19.
Threw a 15-pitch live batting practice at the Nationals’ training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla., on May 15.
Rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Rochester on May 17. Threw one inning on May 17, allowing just two hits.
Continuing his throwing program at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.; signed to a one-year Major League deal with a club option for the 2027 season on May 5.
Throwing bullpen sessions at the Nationals’ training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Continuing throwing progression at Nationals' training complex.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.

Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL..

121.status: Is currently in a 7-10 day strengthening period. Could go on a Minor League rehab assignment as soon as the week of May 25 to see if he can tolerate playing through pain. If not, surgery would become a potential option.

121.injury: Left lat surgery, left hip discomfort
121.status: Restarted rehab assignment May 15 after leaving previous one due to a setback. Will not require full 30-day buildup but will have at least two more outings at Syracuse. Transferred to 60-day IL May 18 (doesn’t affect his timeline).

Flew to Florida to throw a multi-inning live batting practice session on May 17. Will likely throw another one later in the week. Will not advance to game action until his pitch metrics are in the place that he wants.
Began a rehab assignment May 15, almost exactly one month after surgery. Will advance to Triple-A Syracuse soon and build up over the coming weeks.

Fractured his leg on a comebacker May 15. Will need 6-8 weeks to heal and another six or so for a Spring Training-style build-up. That should take him well past the All-Star break in a best-case scenario.
Underwent an MRI on May 13 that showed signs of healing, but not enough for him to begin a running progression. Will not have a concrete timetable until he can restart baseball activities.
Tore his meniscus on a swing in a May 12 game against the Tigers. Underwent surgery on May 14 and is expected to miss eight weeks.

Received an injection April 30 but was still experiencing symptoms as of a May 12 update. Is consulting with outside doctors, though surgery is not on the table. Timeline will remain fluid until symptoms subside.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.injury: Mid-foot bone bruise in right foot
133.status: Will continue his rehab assignment with Triple-A Las Vegas on May 19.
Continues to strengthen and stabilize shoulder before reintroducing baseball activity. No timetable for return.
Began a swing progression on May 18. No timetable for a return.
Shut down from throwing for a minimum of three weeks from the date of May 18.
Shut down from throwing activities at this time due to increase in symptoms. No timetable for return.

134.status: Will start on May 17 for Double-A Altoona in what will be his fourth rehab appearance.
Has resumed all baseball activities and threw a bullpen on May 13.
Sustained apparent injury on May 9 in San Francisco. Foot began swelling during flight to Pittsburgh on May 10. Pirates are still evaluating the seriousness of the infection.

135.status: Fouled a ball off his toe on May 5, sustaining what the team said was a small fracture. Has begun some baseball activities, but isn't pain-free enough to consider a return yet.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery in April 2025. Began a rehab stint on May 7. Had his rehab transferred to Double-A San Antonio on May 17. Is building up as a starter.
Landed on the IL after his start on May 1. Began a throwing program in mid-May after two weeks of rest and recovery.
Placed on the injured list one day after working two scoreless innings in relief at Milwaukee. Timeline TBD.

Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Has played light catch but has recently been shut down entirely.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Initially cleared protocols, but symptoms arose in the days afterward. Has not resumed baseball activity.

Exited April 12 start with elbow stiffness; shut down from throwing. MRI revealed flexor strain. Expected to pitch again this season, though manager Craig Stammen noted Pivetta's absence would last "weeks and maybe months."
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Received a PRP injection for a groin strain, as well as a different injection for an adductor injury. Mariners hope he can return to baseball activities by early June. (last updated: May 17) More »
Making his second rehab appearance with Triple-A Tacoma on May 17. Manager Dan Wilson said they'll see how he feels coming out of it before determining if he's ready to rejoin the club.
MRI revealed Grade 1 strain. Slated to receive cortisone or platelet-rich plasma injection. Was shut down from baseball activity for one week on May 15. Will likely transition rehab to Arizona.More »
Slated for first bullpen session since injury on May 17 after turning corner in catch play during recent road trip. Could begin rehab assignment as soon as May 20.
Will continue rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma through current homestand (through May 20), then could be activated for upcoming road trip.
Injured left groin during a rehab assignment and was shut down on April 18. Is now targeting mid-June to begin fresh rehab assignment.
Recent X-rays showed positive healing, which would lead him to ramp up baseball activity in early June.
Has begun throwing again after experiencing setback during early stages and is making progress.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Removed out of precaution in the fourth inning on May 18 vs. Arizona. Will undergo full assessment May 19 to evaluate severity.
Exited after fifth inning on May 18 vs. Arizona as a result of taking a ball off his thumb. Manager Tony Vitello revealed a contusion but no structural damage.
Scheduled to throw another bullpen on May 19.
Hurt his quad on a sliding catch attempt on May 15. Expected to miss "at least a couple of weeks and probably more than that," per manager Tony Vitello.
Pitched in an Arizona League game on May 13.
Had his rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Sacramento on May 13.
Continuing his swing progression and increasing his baseball activity the week of May 11.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Was removed in the bottom of the seventh on May 17 after legging out a force out. Winn said he felt cramping and was removed as a precaution, but does not expect to miss any time.
Oliver Marmol said that Urías will be retested after taking ground balls on May 17 before beginning his plyo program.
Began his rehab assignment with Single-A Palm Beach on May 15, homering in his first at-bat and drawing a walk in his second, batting second and playing left field vs. Daytona.

139.status: Felt soreness in the sixth inning of his May 3 start, then underwent an MRI on May 4 that revealed inflammation. Imaging showed no structural issues, so he recovered quickly and should start May 20 vs. Orioles.
Was scratched from May 16 lineup and placed on the IL one day later. Manager Kevin Cash said he could require 6-8 weeks of recovery after surgery.
Felt his back "lock up" after traveling from Toronto to Tampa Bay area on May 13-14. Tried to throw off the mound on May 17 but experienced continued discomfort.
Has been sidelined by an unspecified issue in his back and did not start against a left-handed pitcher on May 18. Unclear if he will need an IL stint.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11 then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. Will return to the Tampa Bay area for more imaging. No timeline for return.

A reliever for Triple-A Durham with a spot on the Rays' 40-man roster, Cook was placed on the Minor League IL on May 11. Had a 5.84 ERA with 15 strikeouts and six walks in 12 1/3 innings over 11 outings for Durham.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Scheduled to face hitters for the first time the weekend of May 8-10, with a rehab assignment to follow.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Will need time to build up after being shut down so long.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

After rocky rehab outing on April 15, he reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks from returning.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July.

140.status: Landed on injured list after missing three games in Houston due to back spasms. Was sent for MRI on May 17, which showed back inflammation.

140.injury: Right glute strain, wrist inflammation, viral meningitis
140.status: Diagnosed with viral meningitis while on the injured list with both a glute strain and wrist inflammation. Will be down for at least 7-10 days while recovering in the hospital before Rangers begin a return-to-play program.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but began throwing from 60 feet during the Rangers’ most recent homestand and has been cleared to begin his progression again.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.
Flew from Arizona to join the team in Detroit on May 2, and will not begin ramping up his swing for at least a week, focusing on cardio work and conditioning until cleared to hit.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Only threw one inning in an April 14 loss to the A’s due to arm fatigue that was eventually classified as shoulder impingement.
Left an April 14 appearance after throwing two pitches due to feeling something in his arm. Scans showed minimal issues, though he will be down for at least two weeks.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Continues to catch and hit. Blue Jays should have a timeline by later in the week.
Expected to throw another bullpen session May 19 as he slowly builds up.
Meeting with doctor May 19 to get clearance to begin throwing and hitting.

141.status: Threw 22-pitch bullpen May 18 in New York and will throw again May 20.
Will throw to hitters May 19 in Florida, simulating two innings and 30 pitches.
Will play rehab game with Single-A Dunedin on May 19.
Nance will undergo more tests, but is hopeful that this could be managed with rest. The club should know more later in the week.
Will undergo surgery May 20 to clean up elbow and "loose bodies," but this could also include ligament repair once doctors get a better look.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Suffered injury on a foul ball in the eighth inning on May 18 and was removed from the game. Will undergo imaging on May 19.
Worked out on field again on May 18 and is “very close” according to manager Derek Shelton.
Made rehab start at Triple-A St. Paul. Was removed due to a lengthy inning and finished his throw day in the bullpen, but came through it fine. Next steps to be determined soon.
Has progressed to playing catch at 90 feet.
Made a Minor League rehab appearance at Triple-A St. Paul on Saturday, pitching a scoreless inning with no hits, one walk and no strikeouts.
Played catch on May 12 and May 13, aiming to build up over next week.
Taking light swings and aiming for throwing program May 18. Will have a three-week build-up before a rehab assignment is scheduled.
Had stem cell injection in right shoulder following a rehab setback, will be no throw for three weeks.

142.status: Reporting improvements daily, could resume light baseball activity in a week, pending how each day goes.
Continues to report improvements in muscle strain, but will not resume baseball activities until muscle strain is fully cleared out.
Began throwing last week as far as 105 feet, and could be throwing on a mound again for evaluation by May 17.
Placed on IL on April 28. Last pitched on April 25, allowing one run and striking out two.

143.status: Played catch on May 12 for the first time since going on the IL. Will continue throwing program as he works toward a return.
Initially felt something in his calf while doing agility work on April 14. Began a rehab assignment on May 9, and continues to progress.
On a rehab assignment since April 30. Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique.

Miller was fielding ground balls in April but is no longer doing baseball activities. It might be a setback, although some say the Phillies are being cautious with his recovery. His progress has been slow, and the Phillies have declined to delve into details about it.

144.status: An MRI revealed a strain in Farmer’s right forearm, placing him on the 10-day injured list on May 18.
Ran the bases on May 16. Team expects to activate him when he becomes eligible on May 18. Head slammed into the outfield wall while making a catch on May 10 at Dodger Stadium.

Began throwing bullpens in early May. A timetable hasn’t been set, but he’s not expected to be ready until late June or early July. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow.

Received an encouraging update when he met with doctors in early May. But it’s too early to know when he might be cleared to begin throwing. Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18.
Expected to miss at least eight weeks. Was called for catcher's interference when Hyeseong Kim's bat hit his glove during May 10 game at Dodger Stadium.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Sprained lateral collateral ligament in his right knee on May 16 during rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte. Expected to require 3-6 weeks of rest and rehabilitation before he can return to play.
Will begin a new injury rehab assignment on May 19 with Triple-A Charlotte.
First injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.
White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Manager Will Venable wasn’t sure when Pereira suffered the injury during an April 28 win over the Angels. But it was bothering Pereira both hitting and throwing.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Will visit Dr. Keith Meister on May 21 for further evaluation. Scratched from a start in Minnesota after reporting soreness during a bullpen session May 12. Initial MRI revealed ligament sprain.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

According to manager Clayton McCullough, Conine is running at 70 percent, is hitting and throwing. Trending toward a quicker timeline of return. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.

146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Made sixth rehab start May 16, striking out six over 5 1/3 innings. Threw 86 pitches, 56 for strikes. Club is evaluating if next start could come in Major Leagues.
Manager Aaron Boone suggested Caballero could miss the minimum 10 days. Resumed playing catch May 18. Has vowed to be ready by May 22.

Shut down from throwing for a few weeks. Dr. Neal ElAttrache and team physician Dr. Chris Ahmad agreed on diagnosis and treatment, team spokesperson said May 16. Repeat imaging will be performed when asymptomatic.

Underwent testing May 11 to gauge healing. Manager Aaron Boone said imaging showed the injury "still lingering there." Has been able to "do more dynamic stuff," Boone said May 16, but has not resumed running.
Injured shoulder while crashing into wall making a catch May 7. Received PRP injection May 11. Has been able to swing in a pool and perform weight room activities as of May 16.
Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Will not throw for at least three weeks.

Will not throw for approximately three weeks. Manager Aaron Boone said there is “acute and chronic stuff going on there” that will require rest. Experienced difficulty while throwing on April 25 in Houston.
Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: Simulated two innings in a 30-pitch bullpen May 18 and was so pleased with the results that he said he is trying to push the pace of his comeback. Tentatively, the next step will be to face hitters May 22. (last updated: May 18)

Began another rehab assignment May 16 with High-A Wisconsin and surrendered seven earned runs on five hits, three walks and a hit batsman while recording four outs. Next start is expected to be for Triple-A Nashville. (last updated: May 18)
Currently on rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville. (last updated: May 18)
Sent to Triple-A Nashville on May 15 to begin a rehab assignment. (last updated: May 15)

Slid into a concrete section of the wall while chasing a foul ball on May 8 against the Yankees. All of his scans came back clear for fractures and tears, and he was able to begin riding the stationary bike on May 13. (last updated: May 14)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 12)
Successfully threw a bullpen session on May 12 for the first time since going down with an elbow injury. (last updated: May 12)
